item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't remember where I found the original photo this was posted with, but it probably wasn't CC compliant, so I've decided to swap it out with one that is.

The new picture is aloha hula girl by stereogab (CC BY-SA 2.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A pretty fun SCP. I like the implied backstory, and Tiff doesn't have to be someone like Dr. Wondertainment, who creates anomalies with everything they touch. My mental backstory is that Tiff tried some theoretical hex or gypsy curse, but because she was so angry at her ex, she may have inadvertently supercharged it and created an anomalous item that tries to wreck any vehicle it ends up in, regardless of the driver.

+ Some ideas on expanding the theme.
- Ideas:

I actually had a couple of musings about how to make it such that remote steering wouldn't work for this one, too. The concept stems from punishing the human driver and passengers, so it makes sense it wouldn't work right in a remote controlled vehicle. Maybe have an added option (possibly discovered belatedly and included as a new addendum between A and B) where, when the vehicle accelerates beyond 30km/h and there is no human driver, either:

a. The engine anomalously stalls and will not restart until it slows (or slams) to a stop. Simultaneously, the hula figure teleports to the nearest moving vehicle with a human driver. This would halt all remote testing within one or two tries, and would reinforce the notion that the figure is trying to punish the driver of the vehicle.

b. The nearest human of driving age within a 5 km radius of the vehicle is thrown toward the driver’s seat by an unseen force. Further tests can establish that this radius extends the faster the vehicle goes, and that the next nearest target is selected if the first dies in transit. The driver’s side door may or may not be made to open when the selected human approaches, and this effect may be further complicated if that other individual is currently driving another vehicle.

c. An unseen force assumes control of any remote control mechanism driving the vehicle and proceeds to accelerate uniformly until the other anomalous effects begin to show, with the caveat that the vehicle will avoid all obstacles until it reaches maximum speed for that vehicle. At this point it will target the most life-threatening vehicle or structure nearby and crash, causing as much damage and injury as possible. All attempts to interfere with, stop, or destroy the remote control mechanism will be ineffective until destruction of the vehicle.

Personally, while I like (a), I think (b) is weirdly appealing in an anomalous fashion and is similar to the anomalous effect of a cervine creature being flung at the vehicle beyond 120 km/h.

Or conversely, make it where the figure can tell who is remotely controlling the vehicle, and any damage suffered by the vehicle is inflicted on that individual as if they had been riding inside. Then the Foundation could be justified in using D-class during experiments, because someone's going to be wounded/killed regardless, and they may as well not destroy the additional remote-control equipment installed in the test vehicle.

Anyway, cool idea. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what would happen if it was put in the cab a semi hauling a trailer full of cars?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking the same thing while I was reading the article.

item.title: {no subject}
item.content:encoded:
2 of U = MADE 4 each other. Not even mad. Have fun on the road! — Tiff

+1, for that and much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How in the world is requistioning a D-Class cheaper than adding remote steering to a car? All you have to do is make a few alterations to the controls. Downvoting for unnecessary D-Class usage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens at 140, 150, 160kph? Just curious 8-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My big question is why the Foundation has not sought out "Tiff". A person of interest who seems to have the skill of creating a SCP, this would be a person worth looking into. Aside from that, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet Tiff didn't mean for it to keep going. Unexpected feature of the curse or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, how about making it resilient instead of self-healing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't even imagine the complexity of such a track-back.
It teleports after every crash, and only activates at highway speeds. Keep in mind that a crash on the road drastically slows the traveling speed for hours and sometimes hundreds of miles in both directions. Car #2 could have been half a days drive away from the first accident site before getting up to speed again. Further and longer if it ever gets into one of the rare cars where teh driver obeys the speed limits. Repeat for an unknown period of time before containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So Tiff is mad at her ex and so she wrecks his car, and then keeps going and wrecks all the others too?
I would think the Foundation would look into reports of odd occurances and would have compiled documentation on the history of the object's crashes, and thus discover who "Tiff" was and (if possible) detain her for questioning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if we put Dr. Gerald behind the wheel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it doesn't need to teleport, but it certainly needs to be at least somewhat indestructible. With the amount of trauma this thing sees, we wouldn't have anything to contain if it weren't exceptionally sturdy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm inclined to agree. The backstory of this has this as a tool of revenge, not indiscriminate destruction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Harness the power of passive-aggressiveness!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know you're allowed to fix those yourself, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fun, strightforward SCP, and the backstory adds a lot to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty excellent. I'm a sucker for odd containment procedures that end up seeming logical once you've read the article, and the two deer to be kept on hand definitely qualify. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it. Nicely anomalous. The only thing I personally would change would be removing the teleporting aspect. Not every SCP has to be undestroyable. Still, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty fun. The implied backstory just makes it better. Wholehearted +1 from me, keep up the good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs a little more something…but damn, it threw a moose at me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So-so SCP, but of you add more meat to it, I will give you a +1 to it!

You'll do fine! But I will have to place a Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shoot. Copypasta'ed the wrong version. That's what I get for posting articles at 3 AM. Fixed.

EDIT 2018: The picture used in the article is aloha hula girl by stereogab (CC BY-SA 2.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix the SCP-XXXX's. No vote from me til then.
